label: Nose sinuses etc.
quick_take: Nose and paranasal-sinus cancer is the clearest "vanishing occupational cancer" in CI5 — almost every registry falls, often to near-zero. Absolute rates are low everywhere (<3 per 100 000), so almost every sub-national pattern reduces to small-number arithmetic; we have cut the original finding list back to those with enough cases to stand up.

findings.title: Shanghai men fell 2.94 → 0.35 (−88 %)
findings.text: n=63→131. The urbanisation + workplace-ventilation story familiar from other aerodigestive sites — one of the few registry trends on this site large enough to be statistically solid.
findings.image_alt: CI5 trend chart — Shanghai men fell 2.94 → 0.35 (−88 %)

findings.title: The generalised decline is near-universal
findings.text: Most "biggest decrease" rows are near-zeros; many vol-XII female rates in US-Japanese and US-Chinese subgroups are literally 0.00. This is the signature of a disease class (wood-dust, leather-dust, nickel) that has been regulated out of most workplaces.
findings.image_alt: CI5 trend chart — The generalised decline is near-universal

findings.text: Numbers are small almost everywhere — many of the "rises" are from 0.05 to 0.5 per 100 000, so relative changes exaggerate. Italian provincial M:F ratios (Parma 63:1 etc.), Australian NT Indigenous rates, Iran Golestan female signals, Italy Sondrio +1000 % all sat on single-digit or zero-case cells and were cut from the headline findings on that basis.
The ICD-10 C30–C31 range excludes nasopharynx (C11). Don't conflate with the Asian-Chinese EBV-driven NPC signal.
